text:
There was once an old man who lived alone in an expansive green house. He loved rabbits. Every night he fed them carrot juice. One night the rabbits were thirsty. They gathered near his bed. The old man smiled.

"You know me," he began. "You enjoy your juice. Have it."

The rabbits listened curiously. Drink after drink went down easy. They leaped around feasting their hearts.

The next day was glorious. They stretched their feet. One stomped a flower.

"Good afternoon!"

Everybody stopped.

"You talk?"

"Oh, oh! Just one. Had a little adventure."

"You went out?"

"The fox..."

He grinned. "Ooh! That crafty little thing. Watch."

Fox trotted. She stopped.

"Ho ho!" she snickered.

Then she saw the boot.

"The treasure?" she said.

He shook his head. "Just a silly thing a boy collected."

They talked. Evenings flew.

"So many stars!"

"Yes!"

They
